title: Localize AMD quantized-kernel conformance divergence
hypothesis: The cross-backend AMD token divergence originates in a shared quantized matmul, gather, or reduction assumption and can be found with layerwise parity dumps.

source.evidence: Gemma 4 and Qwen 3.5 fail on RX 7900 XTX with both D3D12 and Vulkan

title: Validate Gemma 4 shared-KV, PLE, and variable-head semantics
hypothesis: Exact shared-KV attention, PLE injection, and per-layer head dimensions will remove Gemma 4 divergence on non-NVIDIA devices.

title: Establish native ORT WebGPU Gemma 4 parity reference
hypothesis: Native ORT WebGPU logits and generated outputs provide a format-independent reference for Backpack ORT and GGUF localization.

title: Study llama.cpp b10068 portable quantized decode kernels
hypothesis: Vendor-portable Q4_K/Q6_K decode techniques from llama.cpp can close Backpack throughput gaps after conformance is stable.

title: Evaluate Modular MAX-style shape specialization and fusion
hypothesis: Shape-specialized fused decode paths can reduce dispatch and intermediate-memory overhead while preserving portable fallbacks.

title: Trace Qwen 3.5 recurrent-state numerical parity on AMD
hypothesis: Layerwise comparison of convolution, delta-net scan, normalization, and recurrent state will isolate the first AMD numerical divergence after startup.

source.evidence: Qwen 3.5 starts after warmup exclusion but produces token salad on RX 7900 XTX

title: Reduce decode dispatch and synchronization overhead
hypothesis: The staged fusion and persistent-state guidance can improve decode throughput after correctness gates pass.
